Here is the law established between the family of the murdered person and the family of the murderer; provision is made that the cities of refuge should be a protection, so that a man should not die for something that was not his willing act. In Christ, the Lord our Righteousness, refuge is provided for those who by faith flee unto him. But there is no refuge in Jesus Christ for presumptuous sinners who continue in their trespasses. Those who flee to Christ from their sins will be safe in him, but not those who expect to be sheltered by him in their sins.

Direction is given to set landmarks in Canaan. It is the will of God that every one should know his own; and that means should be used to hinder doing and suffering wrong. This, without doubt, is a moral precept, and still binding. Let every man be content with his own lot, and be just to his neighbors in all things.

Sentence should never be passed upon the testimony of one witness alone. A false witness should suffer the same punishment which he sought to have inflicted upon the person he accused. Nor could any law be more just. Let all Christians not only be cautious in bearing witness publicly, but be careful not to join in private slanders; and let all whose consciences accuse them of crime flee for refuge without delay to the hope set before them in Jesus Christ.
